prompt_text: Compare Customer.io and Braze for send behavior-triggered emails and push notifications at scale
response:
For **behavior-triggered email and push notifications at scale**, **Braze** is generally the stronger enterprise choice, while **Customer.io** is usually the better fit for teams that want powerful behavior-based automation with simpler setup and lower cost. Both support behavioral email and mobile push, but Braze is consistently described as having stronger mobile push/in-app capabilities and broader enterprise-scale orchestration, whereas Customer.io emphasizes flexibility, developer control, and unlimited event-style activation.[3][5][6]

- **Braze** supports **behavioral email, mobile push, in-app messaging, and SMS** and is positioned for **hundreds of thousands to millions of MAUs** with enterprise-grade scale and broader channel breadth, including channels like RCS, WhatsApp, and LINE.[3][5]
- **Customer.io** also supports **email, push, in-app, SMS, webhooks**, and behavior-based triggers, with a strong focus on workflow flexibility and custom event/attribute-driven campaigns.[2][3][6][9]
- Review data from G2 suggests **Braze is especially strong for push notifications**, while **Customer.io** is praised for **customization** and its **visual workflow builder** for complex logic-based flows.[2]
- For **large-scale mobile-first programs**, Braze is more often recommended because it is designed for **enterprise-scale messaging volume** and is described as particularly strong for **push notifications and in-app messaging**.[3][5]
- For **growth-stage SaaS or product-led teams**, Customer.io is often favored because it is more accessible, offers **transparent pricing**, and supports **unlimited event tracking** without the same kind of data-point billing pressure described for Braze.[3][6]

| Dimension | Braze | Customer.io |
|---|---|---|
| **Best fit** | Enterprise, high-volume, mobile-first engagement | Growth-stage, developer-friendly behavioral messaging |
| **Email triggered by behavior** | Strong | Strong |
| **Push notifications** | Generally stronger at scale | Strong, but less often cited as the top choice for mobile push |
| **Scale** | Built for very large MAU volumes and enterprise orchestration | Scales well, but more often positioned below Braze for massive enterprise use |
| **Workflow complexity** | Powerful, but often requires more planning | Flexible visual workflows and easier setup |
| **Data/event model** | Data-point-based billing noted in comparisons | Unlimited events/triggers emphasized in comparisons |

If your priority is **maximum push performance, mobile engagement depth, and enterprise orchestration at very large volume**, pick **Braze**.[3][5] If your priority is **behavior-triggered lifecycle messaging with faster setup, strong flexibility, and better cost transparency**, pick **Customer.io**.[3][6]
